text.title: WebSocket Client Patterns
text.summary: TypeScript client with reconnect backoff, message queue, heartbeat, and subscribe/unsubscribe lifecycle.

rule.statement: Reconnect with exponential backoff and random jitter. Never reconnect immediately on close.
rule.rationale: Immediate reconnect after a server restart causes a reconnect storm from all clients simultaneously, DDoSing the server. Exponential backoff spreads reconnects over time; jitter prevents synchronized retry waves.

rule.statement: Buffer outgoing messages during disconnect and flush on reconnect. Assign idempotency keys to messages that must not be duplicated.
rule.rationale: Messages sent during a transient disconnect are lost if not queued. Flushing the queue on reconnect ensures delivery without requiring the caller to retry manually.

rule.statement: Set the ping interval below the load-balancer idle timeout. Close the connection if no pong is received within 2 × ping interval.
rule.rationale: AWS ALB default idle timeout is 60s. A 30s ping interval keeps the connection alive. Without heartbeats, stale connections consume file descriptors without the server knowing they are dead.

section.title: Backoff Formula
section.reference: TypeScript WebSocketClient with reconnect, queue, and heartbeat
